reforms.description: Denmark aims to improve public support for business R&D by clarifying objectives and strengthening coordination mechanisms to enhance resource allocation and reduce administrative barriers. Additionally, the country is increasing its R&D tax allowance from 114% in 2026 to 120% by 2028 for R&D spending up to DKK 1 billion, enhancing incentives for research and development activities.
reforms.component_name: Public R&D Support and Tax Allowance
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance resource allocation and incentivize R&D spending, which are expected to raise productivity and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Both reforms are announced for future implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in public R&D support and tax incentives, but are not structural or system-wide.
reforms.source_quote: Public support to business R&D could be improved, as its impact on knowledge production and diffusion, measured by the number of patent applications or citations, is found to be low.

reforms.description: The development of a public digital portal mapping existing test facilities is intended to improve access for SMEs and facilitate collaboration between theoretical research and real-world applications.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to test facilities for SMEs is expected to enhance collaboration and innovation, supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The initiative is announced for future implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative is significant for improving research infrastructure but does not represent a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The development of a public digital portal mapping existing test facilities is welcome as it should improve access, notably for SMEs.

reforms.description: The patent voucher scheme, which partially funds patent applications for SMEs, was reintroduced in 2025, providing financial support for small and medium enterprises to protect their innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating patent applications, the scheme supports SMEs in protecting their innovations, which can lead to increased commercialization and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The scheme was reintroduced and is in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reintroduction of the scheme is significant for SMEs but does not represent a major structural change.
reforms.source_quote: The patent voucher scheme partly funding patent applications of SMEs (up to 75% of costs) was reintroduced in 2025.

reforms.description: The 'Youth Promise' programme will allocate DKK 1.3 billion up to 2035 to fund local initiatives for NEET with a focus on employment and mental health, including funding for Individual Placement Support (IPS).
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing on employment and mental health for NEET individuals, the programme aims to enhance human capital and support economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme is announced for future implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The programme represents a significant investment in human capital but is not a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Youth Promise' programme will allocate DKK 1.3 billion up to 2035, to fund local initiatives for NEET with a focus on employment and mental health.

reforms.description: The 2025 Acceleration Package allocates DKK 246 million to the Kystpuljen national coastal protection fund for the period 2026 to 2028, aimed at enhancing local adaptation projects and reducing flood risks. Additionally, the 2026 Finance Act provides an additional DKK 532 million for local adaptation projects, aimed at enhancing resilience against climate-related risks.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Funding for local adaptation projects is expected to enhance resilience against climate-related risks, supporting sustainable development and economic stability.
reforms.status_evidence: Both funding allocations are announced for future implementation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The funding allocations are significant for local adaptation but do not represent a major overhaul of funding mechanisms.
reforms.source_quote: The 2025 Acceleration Package allocates DKK 246 million to the fund for the period 2026 to 2028.
